description: Green backed Empid with green extending up through nape and crown and down through auriculars. Green auriculars contrasted sharply with white throat. Very narrow white eyering sometimes visible, sometimes not, depending on angle. When seen, had break in upper/forward part of ring. Bright wingbars washed in yellow-green on upper wingbar. Lower wingbar looked white. Tertials black with bright white edging. Yellow-green edges to secondaries. Underparts with yellow wash from undertail coverts well into belly. Modest olive wash on sides of chest. Bill looked chubby from side view. Tail wide and relatively short. Primary extension long. Crown flat (that is rounded, not square or crested). Found by typical Alder "Pleep" call it was giving, which was heard repeatedly as I pursued it in attempts to gain a photograph
